reasoning:
To resolve YES, Wei Yi's FIDE classical rating must increase by at least 1% from its November 9, 2025 baseline. His baseline rating as of late October/early November 2025 is 2754. A 1% increase requires an Elo of at least 2781.54 (effectively 2782), which means he must gain 28 points. 

1) 2025-11-16 (Horizon: 1 week): Official FIDE ratings are published monthly on the 1st of each month. The Wikipedia "FIDE rankings" page mirrors these monthly lists. Since both November 9 and November 16 fall within the same release cycle, the rating on Wikipedia will not change between these dates. Thus, a rating increase is structurally impossible. Estimate: 0.02.

2) 2025-12-09 (Horizon: 1 month): This date falls in the December FIDE ranking cycle. Gaining 28 classical Elo points in a single month at the ~2750 level is exceptionally rare. It would require him to score an astronomical result (e.g., +6 or +7 in an elite super-tournament) against top-tier opposition within one month. Considering we see no ongoing live rating jump of that magnitude, this is highly unlikely. Estimate: 0.04.

3) 2026-02-07 (Horizon: 3 months): By the February 2026 FIDE list, Wei Yi has roughly 3 months of rated classical games to achieve this rating. While more plausible than a 1-month trajectory, ascending from 2754 to 2782 requires him to permanently shift into the world's top 3–5 players in a very brief timeframe. At the highest level, deflation and drew-heavy tournaments make climbing this steep extremely difficult over a short horizon. The probability increases slightly due to time variance, but remains firmly in the bottom tail. Estimate: 0.07.
